note: Declared input. The worked example the procedure was read off of: the part bdo already trusted, decomposed around. tokens change for aesthetic reasons; wiring for interaction reasons; the copy agent for voice reasons edited hourly. Three reasons, three modules, three rates and authorities — the change-axes, not the categories.
anchor: the part I know I like

axis.reason: voice and tone of generated copy

token-port.crosses: the token names and their resolved values; nothing else
token-port.trust: trusted: both sides are deterministic code in one repo
token-port.authority: designer owns the token set; engineer owns the consumption
token-port.change_rate: tokens shift frequently, the port shape rarely

copy-style-port.crosses: the style constraints the agent must honour (max length, casing, palette words)
copy-style-port.trust: untrusted agent output, validated against the constraints before it renders
copy-style-port.authority: designer owns the constraints; bdo owns the prompt that consumes them
copy-style-port.change_rate: constraints weekly; the prompt hourly — the seam holds across the gap
